title: Mellow Velvety Egyptian Tomato Tagine
description: A comforting and healthy Egyptian dish featuring sun-dried tomatoes, perfect for a sick day, with a rich and velvety texture and a hint of bitterness, made with macrobiotic-friendly ingredients. This tagine is slow-cooked to bring out the deep flavors of the tomatoes and spices.

ingredients.name: sun-dried tomatoes
ingredients.notes: packed in oil, drained and chopped

instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped sun-dried tomatoes, vegetable broth, and chickpeas, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the tagine or Dutch oven, and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 25 minutes.
Season the tagine with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
